stories.title: Iran's cultural heritage sites are being damaged by American and Israeli strikes
stories.description: The cost of the joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to Iran's most famous cultural heritage sites.

stories.title: Celebrating Nowruz with mixed emotions
stories.description: Nowruz, the Iranian New Year, is a joyous holiday celebrating spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year, they're balancing joy with anxiety and grief as the war continues.

stories.title: How the Iran war threatens global food supply
stories.description: About a third of all fertilizer shipped globally passes through the Strait of Hormuz. Now shipping is all-but stopped through the Strait and this could have repercussions for the global food supply.

stories.title: Global oil and energy prices are reeling as the war in the Middle East escalates
stories.description: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.

stories.title: Trump says he mulls 'winding down' the Iran war, even as more Marines head to Mideast
stories.description: The USS Boxer group of three ships, carrying thousands of Marines from the 11the Marine Expeditionary Unit, has left California and will reach the Persian Gulf in about three weeks, NPR has confirmed.

stories.title: Why it's so hard for world leaders to bring down oil and gasoline prices
stories.description: From waiving the Jones Act to rerouting oil through the Red Sea, governments are doing their best to make up for the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, but prices are still rising.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street said ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
stories.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
stories.description: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

stories.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
stories.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

stories.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
stories.description: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: Iran: The ‘Mosaic Doctrine’, Zelensky V Orban, Hungary-splaining
stories.description: In a week where assassinations have continued within the top ranks of Iran’s regime, the minister of intelligence, the commander of the Bases paramilitary and the powerful security chief Ali Larijani included, but a weakened government seems to have had little bearing on Tehran’s...

stories.title: No, Iran didn't publish a 'list' of 11 American cities to target
stories.description: A video claiming that Iran published a list of 11 American cities it plans to target has gone viral since it was first posted on Instagram on March 3. We found the origin of this list, and it turns out that it has nothing to do with Iran.

stories.title: Myth of liberation: As war rages, trapped Iranians 'curse' the regime, the Americans & the Israelis
stories.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Borzou Daragahi, award-winning international correspondent and investigative reporter. As Iran marks Nowruz, the Persian New Year, war intrudes into civilian spaces. The psychological terrain is marked less by overt anger than by fatigue,...

stories.title: Iran's Khamenei says enemy 'defeated' in Nowruz message
stories.description: Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei on Friday said the enemies of the Islamic republic were being defeated in the war against the US and Israel in a written message for the Persian New Year, Nowruz. The message was read out on Iranian television, with Khamenei yet to appear pu...

stories.title: 'Trump could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment,' ex-adviser John Bolton says
stories.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, former US national security adviser John Bolton said US President Donald Trump "could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment". Bolton also said he remains mystified by Trump's reversal on regime change in Iran and warned that the Iranian opp...

stories.title: 🔴 Live: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei issues defiant statement on Persian New Year as blasts rock Tehran
Despite decapitation, Iran’s regime endures: Does Hormuz blockade give Tehran the upper hand?
stories.description: William Hilderbrandt is pleased to welcome Paul Taylor, Senior Visiting Fellow in the Europe in the World Programme and a member of the Defence/Security EUrope project. He is also a freelance columnist for The Guardian. Amid the Strait of Hormuz blockade, Mr. Taylor argues that e...

stories.title: Japan's PM walks tightrope in high-stakes Washington visit
stories.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi travelled to Washington hoping to strengthen ties with the United States. The visit turned into a delicate balancing act amid US President Donald Trump's growing pressure on allies to help the US in the Iran war. Yuka Royer discusses the vis...

stories.title: 'Not our war': Palestinians caught in the crossfire of Middle East war
stories.description: Four women were killed in the occupied #WestBank following missile fire from #Iran - the first Palestinian victims of the Middle East #war since it started on February 28. For Palestinians, it is yet another conflict they find themselves caught up in, with little protection.

stories.title: Pezeshkian says Iran not seeking war with Muslim neighbours
stories.description: Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian says his country is not seeking war with its neighbours.

stories.title: India cooking gas crisis forces exodus of textile workers
stories.description: Indian textile workers are returning home after days without cooking gas, as an LPG crisis linked to the Iran war.

stories.title: Military movements indicate Trump is considering Iran ground operation
stories.description: The US is moving military assets to the Middle East that are key to providing support for ground troop operations.

stories.title: Palestinian citizens of Israel demand better protection against missiles
stories.description: At least 15 people have been killed by attacks in Israel since the war on Iran started late last month.

stories.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei says enemy ‘defeated’ in audio message
stories.description: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has released an audio message for the Persian New Year.

stories.title: Will Europe be pulled into the Iran war?
stories.description: Europe is under pressure as Trump pushes allies to support the Iran war. How are they responding?

stories.title: What are the risks of turning energy sites into battlefields?
stories.description: Oil and gas facilities in the Gulf have been attacked since early in the war on Iran.

stories.title: QatarEnergy CEO says warned US, industry officials against attack on energy
stories.description: QatarEnergy CEO Saad al-Kaabi says he warned US of 'consequences' of attacking Iranian energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Trump: No Iranian leaders left to talk to, says war going ‘extremely well’
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said that Iranian leaders are “all gone” and that the White House has “nobody left to talk to.

stories.title: Sri Lanka says it denied US request to land two aircraft at Mattala airport
stories.description: President Anura Kumara Dissanayake says request made on February 26, two days before US and Israel launched war on Iran.

stories.title: Iranians celebrate Persian New Year in first wartime Nowruz in decades
stories.description: Residents of Tehran exchange new year's wishes as US and Israeli attacks continue through the spring equinox.

stories.title: Iran: From Khamenei to Khamenei
stories.description: How Iran’s power structure was built, and how it survives its architect.

stories.title: Explosion hits Jerusalem’s Old City after Iran missile alert
stories.description: An explosion struck Jerusalem’s Old City seconds after sirens warned of an incoming Iranian missile.

stories.title: Iran’s Khamenei says enemy ‘defeated’ in written Nowruz message
stories.description: Iran’s supreme leader, president send conciliatory message to countries in the region as they mark the new year.

stories.title: Could Iran war trigger the next global food shock?
stories.description: From factories to supermarket shelves, the Iran war is disrupting global supply chains.

stories.title: Iran warns of possible attack on Ras al-Khaimah if UAE attacks its islands
stories.description: The General Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces says that it "would attack the source of any aggression against the territory and national sovereignty of the Islamic Republic"

stories.title: Trump admits possibility of quick end to operation against Iran
stories.description: The US president says that Washington is "very close" to meeting its goals in the Middle East

stories.title: US puts Middle Eastern countries in danger instead of protecting them — Iranian MFA
stories.description: Abbas Araghchi says the leaders of Muslim states "should clearly express their rejection of aggression not just in word, but in action"

stories.title: Over 230 US service members wounded since start of Iran conflict — media
stories.description: According to a US official cited by ABC News, most of them sustained traumatic brain injuries

stories.title: Israel conducts two waves of attacks on military targets in Iran
stories.description: Among the targets were "several factories and sites used by the Iranian regime to produce weapons intended to harm the State of Israel and other countries" and sites holding components for long-range ballistic missiles

stories.title: Hezbollah fires ten missile at Karmiel in northern Israel
stories.description: Another Hezbollah target was an Israeli army camp in the settlement of Rosh Pinna in Upper Galilee

stories.title: Russian envoy dismisses report of Moscow proposing intelligence deal to US as fake news
stories.description: Politico claimed earlier that "Moscow proposed a quid pro quo to the US under which the Kremlin would stop sharing intelligence information with Iran if the US ceased supplying Ukraine with intel about Russia"

stories.title: Iran’s late supreme leader repeatedly warned against nuke development — president
stories.description: Ali Khamenei emphasized that nuclear weapons were prohibited for religious reasons

stories.title: Fragment of Iranian missile fell in Old City of Jerusalem, causing damage — Israeli MFA
stories.description: According to the press service of the Israeli Foreign Ministry, the projectile fell "literally a few hundred meters from the Al-Aqsa Mosque"

stories.title: Iran may demand security guarantees involving Russia, China — expert
stories.description: Alberto Bradanini, former Italian Ambassador to Tehran and Beijing, emphasized that if the US truly wishes to end this conflict, a compromise must be reached

stories.title: LNG deficit may be more harmful than oil shortage for global economy
stories.description: According to the report, the conflict in the Middle East "entered a dangerous new phase Wednesday as Israel struck Iran’s South Pars gas field"

stories.title: Lukashenko promises not to supply missiles to Iran during conflict
stories.description: The Belarusian leader noted that Tehran had never never asked Minsk for missiles

stories.title: Trump, US feel EU betrayed them on Iran — Lukashenko
stories.description: As the Belarusian president noted, the US helped the Europeans both with NATO and economically

stories.title: Strikes on Middle East energy facilities could constitute war crimes — Guterres
stories.description: The UN Secretary-General said there were "reasonable grounds" to believe that both Israel and Iran had committed war crimes

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
